preamble: The shift from [l] to [r], often called Lambdacism (when referring to the liquid interchange) or Rhotacism of L, is a common liquid interchange where the lateral airflow is replaced by a central vibration or tap.

examples.note: Neutralization of liquids in coda position.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
